panels.description:
The power factor tells you how much of the supplied power is being used effectively. A value close to 1 (or -1) is ideal; a value much less than 1 means inefficiency.

The negative sign indicates the direction of power flow (possibly exporting power to the grid or reverse flow).

The magnitude shows that the apparent power is being used for useful work.
panels.title: Power Factor (Efficiency)

panels.description:
This gives you the net energy flow (kWh) over the past hour.

Positive value: Net import from grid.
Negative value: Net export to grid.

0.0083333333 = 30s scraping time.
panels.title: Import / Export

targets.legendFormat: bytes of memory are used for other runtime allocations {instance={{instance}}}

panels.description: shows how many heap objects are allocated. This is a counter value so you can use rate() to objects allocated/s.
panels.title: Rate of Objects Allocated

panels.description: go_memstats_lookups_total – counts how many pointer dereferences happened. This is a counter value so you can use rate() to lookups/s.
panels.title: Rate of a Pointer Dereferences
